creator: mickey242@gmx.net
text: A prefix that has been leased by means of dhcp comes with a lifetime by definition. In my case I get a new dynamic prefix every 24 hours with the leases having a lifetime of a little over 2 days. Given the fact that there is already a mechanism in place to propagate this lifetime to the subprefixes assigned to the interfaces, it just doesn't seem right not to so and thereby give other components (i.e. rtadvd) access to the real prefix lifetime instead of one that was completely made up. Also I do not see a problem here, as dhcp6c is still responsible for either renewing the lease or removing the prefixes in time, before the lease expires.
